score_evidence: The first rough ER render was rejected because the sheets were too large, intruded into the nucleus inspection view, and the close-up was mostly a blank sheet surface.
The approved render scales the folded sheets down, moves them outward, smooths the cisterna mesh, and keeps pore/nucleus readability intact.
A follow-up polish pass added dark cisterna edge lips, two visible lumen ribbons, and denser irregular ribosome placement, improving the biological sheet read.
The OpenStax rough ER/nuclear envelope reference and the nucleus envelope baseline are present in the Blender review board.
The final render shows blue folded sheets outside the nucleus with orange ribosome dots, lumen continuity marks, and a bridge from the outer nuclear envelope.
minor_issues: The folded sheets still read more stylized and smooth than the rougher reference membrane network.
The ER lumen ribbons are readable but still slightly bright and infographic-like compared with the source reference.
Ribosome dots are readable but remain simplified rounded markers.
required_fixes: In a later polish pass, add more layered sheet stacking and smaller ribosome density variation.
Tone-map the lumen ribbons before final export so they remain anatomical rather than decorative.
Create Roblox LOD meshes and ribosome texture/instance variants before export.
